image_dependency: question_text only states side lengths and asks for sin B, with no explicit reference to a diagram, labels in an image, or visual attributes
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false suggest upstream image reliance, but the text appears largely self-contained
registry_passed=true and one image exists, so image availability is confirmed even though textual need for it is unclear
multi_step: solving likely requires recognizing the side-length relationship and then mapping side b to angle B before computing the sine
the task is not single lookup, but it is still a short geometry/numeric inference rather than a long chain
answer_type is numeric with no choices, so the solver must derive rather than select
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text=0.96 is a precise, checkable target
question_text gives explicit numeric inputs (a=14, b=48, c=50), enabling deterministic recomputation
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable downstream checking
